title: Purge-aware single-feature IC / regime-conditional information diagnostic
source: GitHub repo scout (4 Haiku explorers) + quant-phd-advisor literature review, 2026-05-31. Conversation: 'search quant trading repositories'.
why: Diagnose whether the tier2 feature set has temporal information content per regime (addresses the 'orthogonal feature set is the real bottleneck' finding, MEMORY hypothesizer_persona_ceiling). quant-phd-advisor: Alphalens' default machinery is CROSS-SECTIONAL and degenerate on a single asset — do NOT use it; build a purge-aware single-feature forward-return IC instead, beside MDA.
value_rationale: VERDICT: Adopt-with-caveats. Moderate value as a regime-information check; diagnostic, NOT a fix (adds no events).
scope: Compute forward-return IC/RankIC per feature per regime UNDER the same purge+embargo and .shift(1) discipline as the pipeline
CRITICAL: a naive IC over overlapping triple-barrier windows REINTRODUCES the look-ahead leak the invariants forbid
Validate vs existing MDA on ema_cross positive control (should rank consistently)
If a purge-aware variant is more effort than extending MDA, prefer extending MDA
recommendation: Adopt-with-caveats as a purge-aware diagnostic only. Do NOT adopt Alphalens' default cross-sectional pipeline (category mismatch on single-asset).
out_of_scope: Alphalens default cross-sectional quantile/RankIC machinery (degenerate on one asset). SHAP as a selector (see below): Skip — not a selector and inherits MDA's substitution bias; if substitution is the real pain use Clustered Feature Importance (CFI), not SHAP.
